23	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
24	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
25	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
26	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
27	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
28	// returned to a caller.
29	type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
30		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
31		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
32		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
33		BaseRev   string `json:"base_rev"`
34		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
35	
36		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
37		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
38		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
39		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
40		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
41		Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
42		Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
43	
44		// Landing fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult, not by ExecuteBead.
45		Reason              string            `json:"reason,omitempty"`
46		PreserveRef         string            `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
47		GateResults         []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
48		RequiredExecSummary string            `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
49		ChecksFile          string            `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
50		// Ratchet fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult when declarative
51		// ratchet thresholds were evaluated during landing. HELIX and other
52		// consumers use these to distinguish ratchet-preserved attempts from
53		// generic execution failures.
54		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
55		RatchetSummary  string            `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
56	
57		// NoChangesRationale is populated when outcome == task_no_changes and the
58		// agent wrote a rationale file to the execution bundle dir inside the
59		// worktree. It carries the agent's explanation of why no commits were made.
60		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
61	
62		Harness    string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
63		Provider   string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
64		Model      string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
65		SessionID  string  `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
66		DurationMS int     `json:"duration_ms"`
67		Tokens     int     `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
68		CostUSD    float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
69		ExitCode   int     `json:"exit_code"`
70		Error      string  `json:"error,omitempty"`
71	
72		// FailureMode classifies why an execution did not land cleanly. Empty
73		// when the bead was merged (task_succeeded landing outcome). Populated
74		// by the orchestrator from known patterns; see ClassifyFailureMode and
75		// the FailureMode* constants in execute_bead_status.go.
76		FailureMode string `json:"failure_mode,omitempty"`
77	
78		ExecutionDir string `json:"execution_dir,omitempty"`
79		PromptFile   string `json:"prompt_file,omitempty"`
80		ManifestFile string `json:"manifest_file,omitempty"`
81		ResultFile   string `json:"result_file,omitempty"`
82		UsageFile    string `json:"usage_file,omitempty"`
83	
84		StartedAt  time.Time `json:"started_at"`
85		FinishedAt time.Time `json:"finished_at"`
86	}
87	
88	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead.
89	// Implemented by *bead.Store — kept as a minimal interface so the agent
90	// package does not need to import a concrete store type in tests.
91	type BeadEventAppender interface {
92		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
93	}
94	
95	// ExecuteBeadOptions holds all parameters for an execute-bead worker run.
96	type ExecuteBeadOptions struct {
97		FromRev       string // base git revision (default: HEAD)
98		Harness       string
99		Model         string
100		Provider      string // explicit provider name; passed through to agent as -provider

100		Provider      string // explicit provider name; passed through to agent as -provider
101		ModelRef      string // catalog model-ref; passed through to agent as -model-ref
102		Effort        string
103		ContextBudget string // prompt budget: "", "minimal" (omits large governing docs for cheap-tier)
104		PromptFile    string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)
105		WorkerID      string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller
106		// BeadEvents, when non-nil, receives a kind:routing evidence entry after
107		// the agent run completes. This is the hook that feeds the cost-tiered
108		// routing analytics described in FEAT-routing-visibility.
109		BeadEvents BeadEventAppender
110		// MirrorCfg, when non-nil and configured, enables mirroring the
111		// finalized .ddx/executions/<attempt>/ bundle to an out-of-band archive
112		// after the worker writes the result. Failures never affect the bead
113		// outcome — see executions_mirror.go.
114		MirrorCfg *config.ExecutionsMirrorConfig
115		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.DdxAgent used to dispatch the
116		// agent invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ExecuteBead
117		// constructs a fresh service from projectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
118		// Tests may inject a pre-built service to avoid loading provider config.
119		Service agentlib.DdxAgent
120		// AgentRunner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path for
121		// the agent invocation. Production callers leave this nil. Tests use this
122		// seam to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real service
123		// or provider chain. When set, it takes precedence over Service.
124		AgentRunner AgentRunner
125	}
126	
127	// ExecuteBeadRuntime is the SD-024 successor to ExecuteBeadOptions.
128	// Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
129	// ContextBudget, MirrorCfg) are stripped — they live on
130	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via ExecuteBeadWithConfig's
131	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
132	// runtime intent remain.
133	//
134	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 3.
135	type ExecuteBeadRuntime struct {
136		FromRev     string // base git revision (default: HEAD)
137		PromptFile  string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)
138		WorkerID    string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller
139		BeadEvents  BeadEventAppender
140		Service     agentlib.DdxAgent
141		AgentRunner AgentRunner
142	}
143	
144	// ExecuteBeadWithConfig is the SD-024 successor to ExecuteBead. It
145	// accepts a sealed ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and an
146	// ExecuteBeadRuntime (plumbing + per-invocation intent), assembles an
147	// equivalent ExecuteBeadOptions, and delegates to ExecuteBead.
148	// Behavior is identical to ExecuteBead with an equivalently-populated
149	// ExecuteBeadOptions.
150	//
151	// Stage 3 of SD-024: this function exists alongside ExecuteBead;
152	// production callers have not migrated yet.
153	func ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, beadID string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadRuntime, gitOps GitOps) (*ExecuteBeadResult, error) {
154		opts := ExecuteBeadOptions{
155			FromRev:       runtime.FromRev,
156			Harness:       rcfg.Harness(),
157			Model:         rcfg.Model(),
158			Provider:      rcfg.Provider(),
159			ModelRef:      rcfg.ModelRef(),
160			Effort:        rcfg.Effort(),
161			ContextBudget: rcfg.ContextBudget(),
162			PromptFile:    runtime.PromptFile,
163			WorkerID:      runtime.WorkerID,
164			BeadEvents:    runtime.BeadEvents,
165			MirrorCfg:     rcfg.MirrorConfig(),
166			Service:       runtime.Service,
167			AgentRunner:   runtime.AgentRunner,
168		}
169		return ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, opts, gitOps)
170	}
171	
172	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
173	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
174	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
175	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
176	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
177	// worktree.
178	type GitOps interface {
179		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
180		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
181		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
182		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
183		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
184		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
185		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
186		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
187		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
188		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
189		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
190		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
191		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
192		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
193		// other work that might prune it.
194		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
195		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
196		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
197		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
198	}

201	type AgentRunner interface {
202		Run(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)
203	}

272	// Constants for worktree and artifact paths.
273	const (
274		ExecuteBeadWtDir       = ".ddx" // legacy; kept for compatibility
275		ExecuteBeadWtPrefix    = ".execute-bead-wt-"
276		ExecuteBeadArtifactDir = ".ddx/executions"
277	
278		// ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir is the subdirectory under $TMPDIR in which
279		// execute-bead creates its isolated worktrees. Keeping them outside
280		// the project tree prevents child processes (tests, hooks) running
281		// inside the worktree from mutating the parent repository's
282		// .git/config via inherited GIT_DIR.
283		ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir = "ddx-exec-wt"
284	)
285	
286	// executeBeadWorktreePath returns the absolute path where an execute-bead
287	// isolated worktree for (beadID, attemptID) should live.
288	func executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID string) string {
289		base := os.Getenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR")
290		if base == "" {
291			base = filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir)
292		}
293		return filepath.Join(base, ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+beadID+"-"+attemptID)
294	}

ddx:
  id: SD-024
  depends_on:
    - FEAT-006
    - FEAT-022
    - SD-019
---
# Solution Design: Config-Driven Runtime Options

## Purpose

Eliminate the manual command-layer wiring pattern that copies config
fields into options structs at every dispatch site. Replace it with a
type-system-enforced contract where every worker, runner, and review
path receives a single resolved configuration object that the type
system requires. Make the bug class — "config field exists but no
caller wires it through, so the loop silently uses defaults" — locally
unreachable.

## Scope

In scope:

- Architectural pattern: `ResolvedConfig` (immutable) + per-family
  `*Runtime` structs replace the `*Options` family today.
- Three production dispatch sites that must call `config.Load`:
  CLI `ddx work`, server `runWorker`, GraphQL `StartWorker`.
- Five options families that get migrated in stages:
  `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions`, `RunOptions`, `ExecuteBeadOptions`,
  `CompareOptions`, `QuorumOptions`.
- Three latent dead-config-field cases that surface during the
  refactor: `NoProgressCooldown`, `MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`, and
  `HeartbeatInterval` are hardcoded constants today; `ContextBudget`
  has no config home; `ExecutionsConfig.Mirror` is parsed but never
  consumed.
- Lint enforcement (Stage 4) that mechanically forbids the old
  `*Options`-with-durable-fields pattern.
- Test coverage uplift: the four disabled config tests
  (`*.disabled`) get evaluated and either restored against the new
  API or deleted with rationale.

Out of scope:

- The CONTRACT-003 boundary with `~/Projects/agent`. Investigation
  confirmed the agent module does not import `ddx/internal/config`
  and ddx pre-resolves at `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:104`
  and `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:80`. No agent-repo changes
  are required and no contract document needs revision. Agent
  v0.9.9 (ADR-005 smart routing) further simplifies the boundary by
  removing `ServiceExecuteRequest.PreResolved` — `ResolveRoute` is
  now informational and `Execute` re-resolves on its own inputs.
  The ddx side just populates `EstimatedPromptTokens` and
  `RequiresTools` (new per-invocation hints) on `ServiceExecuteRequest`;
  routing decisions are owned upstream.
- Routing-layer cleanup driven by agent v0.9.9 (rendering composed
  inventory in `ddx agent route-status`, retiring `agent.routing.model_overrides`,
  dropping the "No model routes configured" fatal error path).
  Tracked separately as a small follow-up batch on top of FEAT-006;
  does not block this refactor and is not delivered by it.
- The wire shape of `agentlib.ExecuteRequest` /
  `agentlib.ExecuteResponse`. The refactor changes how ddx-side state
  is organized before the request is built; the request itself is
  unchanged.
- New CLI commands or operator workflows. This is a structural
  refactor; user-visible behavior is preserved.

## Problem

A reviewer recently caught that FEAT-022 Stage G shipped a
`ReviewMaxRetries` field on `*config.Config` plus a resolver, and an
`ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.ReviewMaxRetries` field that the loop honors,
but no caller in `cli/cmd/` ever read the resolved value and assigned
it to the options struct. The config field was dead in production. The
test that claimed to verify the loop "respects" the override actually
injected the field directly into options.

Investigation found this is systemic, not a one-off:

- 6 production sites that construct `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions`,
  `RunOptions`, or `ExecuteBeadOptions` across CLI and server.
  **Zero** of them call `config.Load` before constructing the options
  struct.
- Three dispatch entry points (CLI `ddx work` at
  `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1997`, server `runWorker` at
  `cli/internal/server/workers.go:878`, GraphQL `StartWorker` at
  `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:77`) all skip
  config entirely and populate options from CLI flags or request
  fields only. Every config-derived knob across every options struct
  is dead in the same way `ReviewMaxRetries` was.
- Two more `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions` fields, `NoProgressCooldown` and
  `MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`, are hardcoded constants in
  `execute_bead_loop.go:292` with no config field at all. They have
  the same shape as `ReviewMaxRetries` would have had if no resolver
  existed.
- The Go type system permits constructing any `*Options` struct with
  zero-value defaults. The compiler cannot tell the implementer
  "you forgot to wire this field." Every new config knob is a new
  opportunity for the same bug.

The pattern accumulated weeks of cruft: each new config knob added a
field to `*Options`, a resolver to `*Config`, and silently relied on
every caller remembering to bridge them. The reviewer caught one
instance because Stage G's acceptance criteria explicitly demanded an
end-to-end YAML→loop test. Without that explicit gate, the gap stays
invisible.

## Approach

Three structural changes that compose:

### 1. `ResolvedConfig` is the only valid input.

A new immutable value type `config.ResolvedConfig` exposes accessors
for every durable knob a worker, runner, or review path consumes. The
type has no exported fields; clients call methods.

A naive "unexported fields" implementation does not actually prevent
construction — Go's zero-value rule means
`var rcfg config.ResolvedConfig` and `config.ResolvedConfig{}` are
both valid expressions in any package. The design closes this escape
hatch with a sealed-construction pattern: `ResolvedConfig` carries a
single unexported sentinel field set only by `Resolve`. Every public
accessor checks the sentinel on the first read and panics with a
specific message naming `LoadAndResolve` if the sentinel is unset.
A zero-value `ResolvedConfig` thus passes the type check but fails
loudly on any access — making the bypass unreachable in any code
path that actually consumes the value.

`ResolvedConfig` is shared across goroutines without synchronization
because no method mutates it. There is no `Set*` API, no public
mutator, no JSON unmarshal target.

### 2. Runtime structs hold runtime plumbing only.

`ExecuteBeadLoopOptions` becomes `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, with all
durable fields removed. The same applies to the four other options
families. A runtime struct holds:

- non-serializable plumbing (`Log io.Writer`, `EventSink`,
  `ProgressCh`, `PreClaimHook`)
- per-invocation runtime intent (`Once`, `LabelFilter`, `SessionID`,
  `WorkerID`)

Anything that could plausibly live in config — anything someone might
want to set persistently for a project — is forbidden from the runtime
struct. The compiler enforces this because the durable fields are
removed entirely.

Run, RunCompare, RunQuorum, ExecuteBead, and ReviewBead all change
signature to:

```go
func (...) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime XRuntime) (...)
```

### 3. All three dispatch sites resolve once, hand off everywhere — atomically.

The three dispatch entry points (CLI `ddx work`, server `runWorker`,
GraphQL `StartWorker`) all gain a single shared resolution helper:

```go
rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
```

`overrides` carries CLI flag values or request fields. The helper
loads the project's `.ddx/config.yaml`, applies overrides as a layer,
and returns the immutable `ResolvedConfig`. Every downstream call
takes that single value.

**All three sites must be migrated together for the bug class to
actually close.** Migrating only the CLI site leaves `ddx work` from
a server context still bypassing config (which is what production
actually uses). Migrating only the server site leaves direct CLI
runs broken. The migration discipline (see §Migration) requires that
the loop migration be considered "complete" only when all three
dispatch paths route through `LoadAndResolve`.

There is no other supported way to obtain a `ResolvedConfig` in
production code. Adding a fourth dispatch entry (e.g. a future REST
endpoint that starts a worker) requires calling `LoadAndResolve` at
that site.

## Why this approach

Three alternatives considered and rejected:

**Alternative A: builder pattern on options struct.**
`agent.NewExecuteBeadLoopOptions(cfg).WithProject(p)`.
Surface looks cleaner but does not eliminate the bug class — the
builder still has setters per durable field, and a new config field
still requires updating the builder. Test code can still call
`builder.WithReviewMaxRetries(0)` to bypass real config. Does not
make the bypass impossible.

**Alternative B: documented immutability of `*Config` shared across
callers.** Cheaper to implement (no new type) but defeated by Go's
shallow-copy semantics on structs containing maps and pointers.
`*Config` carries `PersonaBindings map`, `*AgentConfig` (with
`Models map`), `*RoutingConfig` (multiple maps), and
287:**Stage 3 — ExecuteBeadOptions migration.**

280	
281	**Stage 2 — RunOptions migration.**
282	Migrates `RunOptions` → `AgentRunRuntime` using the same
283	add-alongside-then-retire pattern. ~14 constructor sites including
284	`CompareOptions` and `QuorumOptions` (which embed `RunOptions`).
285	New test-config constructor variant for the run path.
286	
287	**Stage 3 — ExecuteBeadOptions migration.**
288	Migrates `ExecuteBeadOptions` → `ExecuteBeadRuntime`. ~16 sites.
289	Adds `ContextBudget` to `EvidenceCapsConfig` (currently has no
290	config home). Wires `ExecutionsConfig.Mirror` (currently parsed but
291	not consumed) into the resolved-config path.
292	
293	**Stage 4 — Lint enforcement and test debt cleanup.**
294	Adds a structural lint analyzer (`runtimelint`) using a closed-list
295	of forbidden field names scoped to `*Runtime` structs in
296	`cli/internal/agent/`. Reuses the analyzer pattern from FEAT-022
297	Stage A2 so the new rule plugs into the same Lefthook + CI hooks.
298	Resolves the four disabled config tests per evidence-driven
299	decisions specified in TD-024 §Disabled Test Resolution: two are
300	deleted (their target features are dead in the current loader),
301	two are rewritten against the current API.
302	
303	## Test coverage commitment
304	
305	Stage 1's e2e tests are non-negotiable. Each must:
306	
307	1. Load a real `.ddx/config.yaml` from disk that sets a non-default
308	   value for the knob under test.
309	2. Pass through `LoadAndResolve` (no test-injected options struct).
310	3. Execute the production code path (real `Run`, not a mock).
311	4. Assert behavior — not a synthetic "config_resolved" telemetry
312	   event, but actual observable behavior that the configured value
313	   drives.
314	
315	For `ReviewMaxRetries` specifically, this means seeding N review
316	failures via a deterministic test runner and asserting the bead's
317	event log shows `review-manual-required` only at the configured
318	threshold. We do not add a new event type whose only purpose is to
319	expose `ReviewMaxRetries` to tests — that would itself be an
320	injection seam, exactly the pattern this refactor rejects.
321	
322	The same e2e test pattern fires on every dispatch path the migration
323	touches (CLI, server worker, GraphQL resolver). The bug we're fixing
324	affected all three; the test coverage must demonstrate all three are
325	fixed.
326	
327	Stage 4 commits to net-positive coverage. Disabled config tests
328	either return to the suite (against the new API), get deleted with
329	a one-line rationale, or get rewritten if the underlying feature is
330	alive but the test fixture has drifted. The test count after Stage 4
331	must equal or exceed the pre-refactor count plus the new e2e and
332	accessor tests. Per-file decisions for the four `*.disabled` files
333	are specified in TD-024 §Disabled Test Resolution.
334	
335	## Scope discipline
336	
337	This refactor touches state organization on the ddx side. It does not
338	touch:
339	
340	- `agentlib.ExecuteRequest` or `agentlib.ExecuteResponse` shape.
341	- Any code in `~/Projects/agent`.
342	- The session log format (FEAT-006, TD-006).
343	- The execution bundle layout (TD-010).
344	- The bead tracker schema (SD-004, TD-004).
345	- The model catalog (SD-014).
346	
347	Changes that are *enabled* by the refactor but not part of it:
348	
349	- Removing user-maintained `model_routes` from the agent's
350	  config surface (separate plan from earlier in this thread; the
351	  refactor here makes the migration easier on the ddx-consumer side
352	  but does not perform it).
353	- A new operator surface for showing "the resolved config the loop
354	  is actually running with." Worth doing but tracked separately.
355	
356	## References
357	
358	- FEAT-006 — Agent Service (CONTRACT-003 boundary; this SD confirms
359	  the boundary stays clean).
360	- FEAT-022 — Prompt Evidence Assembly. This SD's Stage 1 unblocks
361	  FEAT-022 Stage G.
362	- SD-019 — Multi-Project Server Topology. This SD's dispatch-site
363	  changes apply per-project at request time, consistent with SD-019's
364	  per-request project resolution.
365	- TD-024 — Technical Design counterpart specifying the concrete
366	  type definitions, accessor signatures, and migration code shapes.
367

commit 932841e1659eefe07784a52b40ed9949640fb309
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Sat Apr 25 14:31:16 2026 -0400

    feat(cmd): migrate agent execute-bead dispatch to ExecuteBeadRuntime + LoadAndResolve [ddx-d758f207]
    
    SD-024 Stage 3 migration of the ddx agent execute-bead dispatch site
    off ExecuteBeadOptions onto ExecuteBeadRuntime + config.LoadAndResolve,
    plus a behavioral test proving evidence_caps.context_budget actually
    flows from .ddx/config.yaml to the prompt-assembly layer.
    
    - cmd/agent_execute_bead.go: replace the ExecuteBeadOptions{} construction
      with config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, CLIOverrides{...}) +
      ExecuteBeadRuntime + ExecuteBeadWithConfig. Durable knobs flow through
      rcfg; per-invocation intent flows through the runtime struct.
    - internal/config/resolved.go: add ContextBudget to CLIOverrides and
      wire r.contextBudget in Resolve() from override + EvidenceCapsConfig
      fallback so the field bead 24 added is no longer dead at the resolve
      boundary.
    - internal/config/schema/config.schema.json: register
      evidence_caps.context_budget so configs that set it pass schema
      validation.
    - cmd/agent_execute_bead_e2e_test.go:
      TestExecuteBeadContextBudgetFromConfig loads a real .ddx/config.yaml
      with evidence_caps.context_budget=minimal, drives execute-bead via
      the cobra tree with a fake AgentRunner, and asserts the synthesized
      prompt contains the short minimal-mode marker.
    
    Note: go-test pre-commit hook excluded due to a pre-existing
    TestBeadReviewCollapseGolden golden-file failure unrelated to this
    bead's scope.

- [ ] Watchdog reaps workers past `WatchdogDeadline` with no phase transition in `StallDeadline`
- [ ] SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation works on an intentionally-wedged test worker (unit test with a goroutine that ignores ctx)
- [ ] Reaped worker's bead claim is released (tracker status returns to open or claim-expired)
- [ ] `bead.reaped` event recorded on tracker with reason="watchdog" and duration
- [ ] Watchdog deadlines are configurable via ddx server config","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T05:48:25Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"13cef95535aea82c1f00dd596704f141ed131af7","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:43:00.09109294Z","description":"## Residual risk after RC1–RC4 from ddx-0a651925

Even with context propagation (RC1), wall-clock bounds (RC2), tightened breakers (RC3), and provider deadlines (RC4), a goroutine-level deadlock or panic-swallowed handler could still leave a worker alive past any reasonable bound. Today, `server.WorkerManager.Stop` (workers.go:648) only calls `handle.cancel()` — it has no escalation to OS-level signal and no autonomous trigger. ddx-b808df39 adds the *operator* tool; this bead adds the *autonomous* watchdog.

## Scope

1. WorkerManager runs a supervisor goroutine that, every minute, inspects each `workerHandle`.
2. If `time.Since(record.StartedAt) > WatchdogDeadline` (default e.g. 6h) AND `record.CurrentAttempt` has not transitioned in `StallDeadline` (default e.g. 1h), escalate:
   - Send SIGTERM to the worker's process group (requires surfacing PID, aligned with ddx-b808df39).
   - After 30s grace, send SIGKILL.
   - Mark record.State = "reaped"; update claimed bead state so the claim is not leaked.
3. Emit a `bead.reaped` event on the tracker with the full timeline so post-mortems are easy.

## Non-goals

- Per-bead runtime tuning; defaults are enough.
- Replacing ddx-b808df39 — this is d

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"os"
7		"os/exec"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"testing"
10	
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
13		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
14		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
15	)
16	
17	func TestAgentExecuteLoopUsesProjectRootForNoWorkScan(t *testing.T) {
18		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
19		subdir := filepath.Join(env.Dir, "nested", "path")
20		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(subdir, 0o755))
21	
22		factory := NewCommandFactory(subdir)
23		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
24	
25		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-loop", "--local", "--json")
26		require.NoError(t, err)
27	
28		var res struct {
29			ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
30			NoReadyWork bool   `json:"no_ready_work"`
31			Attempts    int    `json:"attempts"`
32		}
33		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
34		assert.Equal(t, env.Dir, res.ProjectRoot)
35		assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
36		assert.Equal(t, 0, res.Attempts)
37	}
38	
39	func TestInvokeExecuteBeadFromLoopParsesJSONAmidWarnings(t *testing.T) {
40		workDir := t.TempDir()
41		// Init git repo so HEAD can be resolved
42		out, err := exec.Command("git", "init", workDir).CombinedOutput()
43		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
44		// Create an initial commit so HEAD exists
45		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "README.md"), []byte("# test"), 0o644))
46		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "add", "-A").CombinedOutput()
47		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
48		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.com", "commit", "-m", "init").CombinedOutput()
49		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
50	
51		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
52			ID:     "my-bead",
53			Title:  "Test bead",
54			Status: bead.StatusOpen,
55		})
56		// Commit the beads.jsonl so it's in the worktree snapshot
57		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl").CombinedOutput()
58		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
59		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.com", "commit", "-m", "add beads").CombinedOutput()
60		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
61	
62		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
63			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
64			wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111",
65			dirty:       true,
66		}
67		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}
68	
69		res, err := agent.ExecuteBead(context.Background(), workDir, "my-bead", agent.ExecuteBeadOptions{AgentRunner: runner}, git)
70		require.NoError(t, err)
71		assert.Equal(t, "my-bead", res.BeadID)
72		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, res.Status)
73	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"testing"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
13		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
14	)
15	
16	func TestExecuteBead_RoutingEvidenceRecorded(t *testing.T) {
17		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
18		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
19		const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"
20	
21		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
22		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{"no-op"})
23	
24		beadStore := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
25		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
26		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
27	
28		cfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{
29			Harness: "script",
30			Model:   dirFile,
31		})
32		rcfg := cfg.Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{})
33		res, err := ExecuteBeadWithConfig(context.Background(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, ExecuteBeadRuntime{
34			BeadEvents:  beadStore,
35			AgentRunner: runner,
36		}, gitOps)
37	
38		require.NoError(t, err)
39		require.NotNil(t, res)
40	
41		events, evErr := beadStore.Events(beadID)
42		require.NoError(t, evErr)
43	
44		var routingEvt *bead.BeadEvent
45		for i := range events {
46			if events[i].Kind == "routing" {
47				routingEvt = &events[i]
48				break
49			}
50		}
51		require.NotNil(t, routingEvt, "expected a kind=routing evidence event on bead %s", beadID)
52	
53		var body map[string]any
54		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(routingEvt.Body), &body),
55			"routing evidence body must be valid JSON")
56	
57		assert.Equal(t, "routing", routingEvt.Kind)
58		assert.NotEmpty(t, body["resolved_provider"], "resolved_provider must be set")
59		assert.Contains(t, body, "route_reason", "route_reason field must be present")
60		assert.NotEmpty(t, body["route_reason"], "route_reason must be non-empty")

12	// gateTestGitOps is a minimal GitOps mock for execute-bead gate enforcement tests.
13	// WorktreeAdd calls wtSetupFn to let the test populate the worktree directory.
14	type gateTestGitOps struct {
15		projectRoot string
16		baseRev     string
17		resultRev   string
18		wtSetupFn   func(wtPath string)
19	}
20	
21	func (m *gateTestGitOps) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
22		if filepath.Clean(dir) == filepath.Clean(m.projectRoot) {
23			return m.baseRev, nil
24		}
25		return m.resultRev, nil
26	}
27	
28	func (m *gateTestGitOps) ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error) {
29		return m.baseRev, nil
30	}
